questionHtml:
State whether your result from part (b)(ii) indicates there is sufficient evidence to claim that, at the 5% significance level, X and Y are not linearly correlated.

Give a reason for your answer.
markschemeHtml:
0.0325<0.05          R1

(so we reject H0 in favour of H1)

(there is sufficient evidence to suggest) X and Y are (linearly) correlated         A1

 

Note: Their conclusion must be consistent with their p-value and their hypotheses and it must be in context.

 

[2 marks]
